Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL is DENIED. Assuming that this motion is timely, Plaintiff failed to establish any basis for a new trial.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion For Reconsideration And Motion To Vacate Judgment Based On Ada Violation
Off Calendar Nov-05-2025 Off Calendar

PLAINTIFF KARENA FENG'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT BASED ON ADA VIOLATION

is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice CCP 1005 and 1010.6.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED without leave to amend for the reasons stated in the moving papers. Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Plaintiff's late-filed opposition is considered. Plaintiff fails to suggest any possible amendments that could cure the issues raised in the moving papers regarding the events that took place in April 2015 (per Plaintiff's complaint in this case) and addressed in 2019 Federal case. The Court notes that Plaintiff filed a non-confidential request for a reasonable accommodation. The request is granted as follows: (1) the motion is being ruling upon on the papers only; there shall be no oral argument; (2) the tentative ruling shall be e-mailed to the Plaintiff (afengre@gmail.com) by the clerk of the Court; (2) Plaintiff is excused from in-person appearance for October 29, 2025 hearing. The request is otherwise denied.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported.
(D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KEVIN MARTIN DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Off calendar for failure to comply with meet and confer laws. Austin declaration fails to show that the parties met by phone or conference as required by CCP 430.41. A response to the complaint is now due by September 8, 2025.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
